Contrast on raised surfaces — seven options

Markup verbatim from SettingsRow.tsx:26-38 and StatusBar.tsx:756-863. Glass themes render their real wallpaper at their real panels-opacity / panels-blur. Left panel is always Today; right panel switches.

The number everyone has been measuring is the wrong number. Every contrast tool in this workspace compares fg-muted to the flat inset token. On a glass theme the text is actually painted on 50% inset over {panels-opacity{'}'} panel over a blurred photograph. In Meadow Mist that drops the row subtitle from a measured 2.01 to an as-painted 1.83, and it changes as the wallpaper moves. Options B and C cannot fix that — they only move flat tokens. Only E (derive against the composite), G (don't stack surfaces) and I (make the backing opaque) engage with it.
Option
fg-muted→fg-dim, fg-faint→fg-muted inside overlays. D does it by redefining --fg-muted on the subtree (no specificity fight, hovers intact); A does it by overriding color (needs the :not(:hover) split for 83 sites). Identical pixels, very different risk.
Meadow Mist glass 58% / 22px blur · wallpaper avg #527C9E muted on flat inset 2.01 → as actually painted 1.83
Today

Look at: the third row (“Last synced…”) as the wallpaper shifts behind it — that subtitle sits on 50% inset over 58% panel over a blurred photo. Option I is the only one that stops the photo showing through it.

How each option scores

OptionFixes glass themesTheme editsCall-site editsCascade riskHelps future community themes
A descendant colorpartlynonenonehigh — 83 hover sitesyes
D redefine variablepartlynonenonenoneyes
B repaletteno7 themesnonenoneno — each author must
C tighten ladderno3 themes, heavynonenoneno
E derive per surfaceyesnonenonenoneyes, automatically
G fix call sitesyesnone~33 + tailnoneyes
I opaque backingyesnone1 CSS rulelowyes

Fidelity notes

Verbatim: SettingsRow + StatusBar class strings, .layer-surface from globals.css:811-821, wallpaper-glass rule from globals.css:822-824, all token values, Meadow Mist’s real radius-lg and Nunito font stack. Real: wallpaper images, backdrop-filter blur, panels-opacity. Approximated: icons are glyphs not SVGs; StatusBar shown without surrounding chrome; particle effects and vignette omitted (Cotton Candy Sky has dust particles + 0.18 vignette which would darken it slightly further); as-painted ratios use the wallpaper’s average luminance, but the p5–p95 range on Meadow Mist is 0.019–0.768, so local contrast swings well beyond the single number shown.
